source: Isaac Lab RSL-RL PPO, task Isaac-Velocity-Flat-RobotDog-v0 (8-DOF), 1500 iters, GB10 (seed 42), REALISTIC PHYSICS (0.7kg, 0.2 N*m MG90S servos)
physics_note: Trained on corrected physics (mass 2.1->0.7kg, servo effort 2.0->0.2 N*m). mean reward 27.7 (vs 41.6 on the old over-torqued model). The robot is UNDERPOWERED at realistic torque: it learns a timid, stable crawl (dof_torques~0) rather than brisk walking. MuJoCo: stays upright 1000 steps but only ~0.1 cm/s forward at cmd 0.17. This matches the real robot's slow shuffle — the sim now reflects reality. Brisk RL walking would need stronger servos or closed-loop sensing.
morphology: 4 legs x (abduction + knee); right-side abduction axis mirrored

verified.note: tracks commanded base velocity within ~0.08 m/s, never fell over 400 steps x 64 envs

deploy_confirmed.negative_control_no_remap: falls at step 7 — confirms the isaac<->deploy joint remap is correct and load-bearing
deploy_confirmed.hardware: 60 q-frames streamed to robotdog8.ino over serial @~18Hz; all frames parsed, servos tracked final pose (transport+framing validated; gait quality pending IMU)

obs_frame_note: base_lin_vel/base_ang_vel are in the ROBOT BASE frame; projected_gravity is gravity rotated into the base frame. On hardware, supply these from an IMU.
remap_note: ONNX obs joint sub-vectors and the 8 action outputs are in joint_order_isaac (type-grouped). To send to the ESP32 (servo_map channel order = joint_order_deploy / robotdog8.ino ch0-7): deploy[k] = isaac[isaac_to_deploy_index[k]]. To build obs joint sub-vectors from per-leg/servo state: isaac[k] = deploy[deploy_to_isaac_index[k]]. Right-side abduction is axis-mirrored in the model; the hardware also mirrors via servo dir (-1 on FR/RR) in robotdog8.ino.
